signals.reasoning: Hold the live SOL short for now, but only as a tight management hold. The position is finally green again, is retaining 100% of current peak unrealized P&L, and SOL is trading slightly below entry instead of immediately running away. Confidence stays capped because the board is still under a VETO headline, the broader recovery tape is not truly bearish yet, and there is still no stop with only about 1% liquidation distance.
BTC is still holding most of the squeeze recovery and remains directly tied to the live Hormuz-driven headline regime. Under VETO, this is not the spot to force a new directional call.
ETH is still structurally firm and not breaking down in a way that cleanly supports fresh shorts, while the same live event brake still blocks chasing strength. Best left neutral.
XPL continues to recover, but it is already extended enough that a fresh long would be late and a fresh short would be fighting the tape. The live veto keeps it watch-only.
LDO has not rolled over enough to justify a fresh short and still sits inside a rebound structure that is too messy to buy cleanly into a live event regime. No action.

IO is another rebound name with some underlying strength, but positive funding and a live geopolitical brake make it too messy for a fresh call. It is not cleaner than simply managing the existing SOL position.
MORPHO remains one of the steadier underlying recoveries on the sheet, but it still lacks enough independent edge to override the event veto. Watch, do not force.
TST is already too expanded after the squeeze and open-interest surge to trust for a clean fresh entry. Under VETO it remains pure watchlist material.
KNEIRO still lacks robust live chart context in this snapshot and remains too thin and underdefined to trade confidently in an event-driven tape.
BOME is already off its highs and still noisy enough that neither chasing nor fading looks clean. With the board under VETO, the disciplined answer is to stand aside.
